title: veronica rosea subsp. chartonii
intro: Veronica rosea subsp. chartonii, also known as alpine speedwell, is a plant species within the family Plantaginaceae, characterized by its small, blue flowers and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Veronica rosea subsp. chartonii include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
